MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hal Chase, Esq. 415-453-0555 of Vasquez Estrada Conway for Plaintiff.
David Delbon, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3962 of Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CCSF.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff states factes, argues design immunity, dangerous condition, there is a triable issue of material fact, Cornette case, area of high vehicle impact. Defendant argues and inquires re: videos. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative rulinf as follows:

DEFENDANT (CONSOLIDATED CASE) CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant and Cross-Complainant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Taking the facts and evidence in the light most favorable to plaintiff, the Court finds no triable issue of material facts that a dangerous condition existed at the time of the incident. When the immediate cause of the injury is the illegal act of a third party (such as the running of the red light by Mr. Perez), the City's liability under Gov't. Code Sec. 835 only exists when a feature of the public property has increased or intensified the danger to users from third party conduct. (Song X. Sun v. City of Oakland (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1177, 1187.) Here, there is no admissible evidence that the all red interval increased or intensified the danger to users from third party conduct. The Court further finds that taking the evidence and facts in the light most favorable to plaintiff, there are no triable issues of material fact regarding the City's affirmative defense of design immunity under Gov't Code Sec. 830.6. The City presented uncontroverted evidence that the city traffic engineers had the delegated discretionary authority to approve signal design and timing. The City further presented evidence that there was a prior approval of the all-red signal and that all features, including signal light timing, conformed to accepted engineering standards and were reasonable. The declaration of plaintiff's expert Mr. Ruzak does not create a triable issue of material fact as to either element of design immunity. (CACI 1123 and Grenier v. City of Irwindale (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 931, 941-942.) Finally, the Court finds that there are no triable issues of material fact as to whether changed physical conditions defeat design immunity. Plaintiff presented no evidence that the alteration of the physical conditions (i.e. the enhanced safety feature of an "all-red" phase) rendered the design change dangerous. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Off Calendar May-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Alejandro Melgarerejo-Medina's motion for leave to file amended answers is granted. Mr. Medina is granted leave to file amended answers by May 23, 2017. California has a liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. Plaintiffs Catherine Wendling, Marc Wendling and Luca D'Isanto have not shown the proposed new affirmative defenses are shams. Any noncompliance with CRC 3.1324 by Mr. Medina is of no consequence and not a basis to deny this motion. Nor have plaintiffs shown that they will suffer legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Plaintiffs' primary opposition to the motion on the grounds that they properly rescinded the settlement agreements with Mr. Medina is not amenable to resolution on this motion or a pleading motion such as a demurrer and will need to be resolved based on an evidentiary motion such as a motion for summary adjudication or at trial.
